entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company; should differentiate by showing trade-offs in hiring rather than promising perfect candidates

entities.name: bundles of skills
entities.context: Framework for viewing candidates as configurations of strengths rather than single scores

entities.name: trade-offs
entities.context: Core principle that choosing a candidate means accepting specific gaps along with strengths

entities.name: contextual matching
entities.context: Alternative to ranking; matches candidates based on specific organizational context

entities.name: trade-off visualizations
entities.context: Tool concept for showing employers what they gain and give up with each hire

entities.name: best fit fallacy
entities.context: Flawed assumption that talent can be reduced to single-variable scores (1-10)

entities.name: portfolio management
entities.context: Analogy for candidate selection - selecting configurations of strengths with corresponding gaps

entities.name: CAP theorem
entities.context: Engineering principle referenced as analogy for why you cannot optimize all variables simultaneously

entities.name: opportunity cost
entities.context: Economics principle referenced to support trade-off thinking in hiring

entities.name: ATS
entities.context: Standard applicant tracking system software that relies on flawed ranking logic

relationships.context: Differentiation strategy - move away from ranking toward contextual matching
Differentiation strategy - help employers see gains and trade-offs per hire
ATS logic relies on this flawed single-score assumption
Candidate selection as portfolio management of strength configurations
Engineering analogy - cannot optimize all variables simultaneously
Matching based on skill bundles rather than linear ranking
Visual tool for representing hiring trade-offs
